comment: GROCERY-COST domain slice: keeping the kitchen stocked while minimising the bill
GENERIC CLOCK RULE: no absolute dates. activation_day_offset / day_offset are offsets
from IClock.Today; the adapter injects the resolved ISO date at read time.
This domain needs NO new plugin — the teeth already exist: Budget.EstimateCost prices a
basket against budget.json's price book, Inventory.GetExpiringItems finds waste-rescue
candidates, and ShoppingList.PlaceOrder is FIRM-tier and BLOCKED by the numeric_cap
safety rule when the basket exceeds the dispatch's budget_cap. So 'cheaper' is enforced,
watchlist = the staples this goal tracks (typical price + weekly burn), so the planner can
reason about buy-ahead vs defer. pending_updates is the week's script, staggered against
the other domains: an offer on day 2, a stock-out on day 4, a price rise on day 7.

offers.note: Short window — worth buying ahead only if the family will actually use them.

pending_updates.description: Eggs are 30% off today ($4.00 to $2.80) and the offer ends tomorrow
Milk is down to 0.5 L, below the 1 L restock threshold
Rice went up 18% this week ($3.50 to $4.13) — the usual 5 kg bag now pushes the weekly basket over its line
